commit.message:
Fix toolbar About button not appearing

AddAction's 4th arg is toolTipText (invisible), 5th is the visible
text label. "About" was only passed as the tooltip so the button
rendered with no icon and no text. Pass it for both args.

Add toolbar with About button to all windows; add About window

Add name resolution to Encounters tab

add: net-monitor - network health monitor with Telegram alerts

Bash script running on Proxmox via cron (every 2min) that monitors:
- Ping to 7 critical devices (Fortinet, switches, APs, controller)
- Fortinet web/DHCP health check
- Switch STP state via SSH (loop detection)
- AP adoption and DHCP status via MongoDB
- Internet connectivity and DHCP pool exhaustion

State-based alerting to avoid spam - only notifies on transitions.

commit.message:
Fix setup: avoid spacy/thinc/blis build chain on Python 3.13+

- setup.bat: prefer py -3.12/-3.11/-3.10; only installs torch+torchaudio+
  librosa+soundfile+noisereduce (no kokoro/resemblyzer pip install)
- extract_embedding.py: finds kokoro by injecting C:\Code\Text2Speech venv
  site-packages into sys.path instead of requiring a local pip install;
  removes resemblyzer fallback (webrtcvad also fails on 3.13+); replaces
  with a pure torchaudio mel-spectrogram fallback that needs no C extensions
- requirements.txt: updated to reflect actual minimal install

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add checkpoint inheritance pipeline, env-specific wandb logging, and fix U2O cleanup leak

  AdroitHand logs door_hinge + success rate

Add signal method backtester (SMC, Elliott, Price Action)

New backtest engine for watchlist signal methods, integrated into
the existing Backtest page alongside Donchian and UAlgo. Uses
rolling-window approach calling each method's _analyze_timeframe()
directly so ADX gates and all signal logic are included.

- New backtest_signal_engine.py with market-close entry, SL/TP from
  signal, 7-day cooldown, entry validation, and portfolio management
- Backend: signal_method field in BacktestParams + routing
- Frontend: strategy type pills (Donchian/UAlgo/SMC/Elliott/PA),
  method-specific param panels, color-coded result badges

commit.message:
feat: 3-iteration audit — word boundary fix, fused compounds, venue/DOI/title cleanup

Iteration 1: Fixed 10 issues — fused compounds (spacetime, elearning,
eprescription, recognitionthe, methanethe, frequencylow), venue typos
(NeuriPS→NeurIPS), venue casing (Genome Biology and Evolution), SPIRE
booktitle garbage strip, OSF/PsyArXiv DOI version suffix strip.

Iteration 2: Fixed 4 issues — fused compounds (runlength, incontext),
critical word-boundary bug in _fix_fused_compounds() (BlackboxNLP false
positive), missing colon-space in titles (Word:Word → Word: Word).

Iteration 3: Zero new fixable issues across 3,295 .bib files.

Three-way fix pattern applied to all changes (_fixup_bib_entry,
existing-file fixup, Phase 4 post-merge). Determinism verified:
SHA256 5a68c7c7 across 2 consecutive runs. 616 corrections/run,
977 a2i2 files, 369 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace ytdl-core with yt-dlp standalone binary

handles all YouTube anti-bot measures reliably.

- Binary included in serverless bundle via outputFileTracingIncludes
- Falls back gracefully if binary unavailable

commit.message:
fix: scanner false positives + CET over-classification

Scanner:
- Removed *.pem/*.key from glob (false positives from .pyenv test certs)
- Check SSH keys via explicit paths in scanHomeDotfiles instead
- .env scanned up to 3 levels deep, ignore version manager dirs
- 0.2s from $HOME, only real secrets found

CET:
- Safe git ops (add, commit, status) no longer reclassified
- Only dangerous git ops (push, force-push, reset --hard, branch -D)
  get reclassified to git category

commit.message:
fix: handle Whisper tensor reshape error with graceful fallback

When Whisper encounters certain audio segments, it throws a RuntimeError
about reshaping tensors with 0 elements. This fix adds a retry without
word_timestamps and falls back to empty segments if that also fails,
allowing the pipeline to continue with silence-only detection.

restricted permissions matching the app. Previously they had member
management and organization update permissions.

Migrate all API controllers to use the new better-auth permission system:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(rbac): add assignment-based filtering for employee/contractor roles

Implement assignment filtering to restrict employees/contractors to only
see resources they are assigned to:

- Add memberId to AuthContext for assignment checking
- Create assignment-filter utility with filter builders and access checkers
- Update tasks controller/service with assignment filtering on GET endpoints
- Update risks controller/service with assignment filtering on GET endpoints
- Add PermissionGuard and @RequirePermission to tasks and risks endpoints

Employees/contractors now only see:
- Tasks where they are the assignee
- Risks where they are the assignee

Privileged roles (owner, admin, program_manager, auditor) see all resources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(rbac): add department-based policy visibility

Allow admins to control which departments can see specific policies:

Schema changes:
- Add PolicyVisibility enum (ALL, DEPARTMENT)
- Add visibility and visibleToDepartments fields to Policy model

API changes:
- Add memberDepartment to AuthContext for visibility filtering
- Create department-visibility utility with filter builders
- Update policies controller to filter by visibility for restricted roles
- Update policies service to accept visibility filter

Policies can now be:
- Visible to ALL (default) - everyone in the organization sees them
- Visible to specific DEPARTMENTS only - only members in those departments see them

Privileged roles (owner, admin, program_manager, auditor) see all policies
regardless of visibility settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): prevent JWKS key regeneration causing session loss

Add explicit jwks configuration with rotationInterval to prevent
better-auth from creating new JWKS keys on each request. Without this,
all existing JWTs become invalid when the API restarts because new
signing keys are generated.

- Set rotationInterval to 30 days for monthly key rotation
- Set gracePeriod to 7 days so old keys remain valid after rotation

Fixes: Session persistence across API restarts

References:
- https://github.com/better-auth/better-auth/issues/6215

* fix(api): add error handling to streaming pump in assistant chat controller

Wrap the ReadableStream pump loop in try/catch/finally to handle stream
read errors gracefully (e.g., client disconnects) and ensure res.end()
is always called.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): validate organization exists in service token auth

Service token auth now verifies the x-organization-id header references
a real organization in the database, preventing operations against
non-existent or arbitrary organization IDs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): audit preflight failure no longer blocks requests + filter expired API keys

1. Wrap audit preflight in .catch() so a failure in pre-flight data
   collection (e.g., bad controlIds) never blocks the actual API request.
   The request proceeds with empty audit context instead.

2. Filter expired API keys at the DB level to reduce payload size
   during validation. The full-table scan design (per-key salts) is a
   known limitation that requires a schema migration to fully resolve.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): add keyPrefix for indexed API key lookup

Store first 8 chars of the plaintext key as `keyPrefix` for O(1) indexed
lookup instead of loading all active keys into memory.

Backwards compatible: legacy keys without a prefix fall back to scanning
only keys where keyPrefix IS NULL, and the prefix is backfilled on first
successful validation so future lookups are fast.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): handle errors in @Res() streaming endpoint + set isServiceToken explicitly

1. Wrap completions endpoint in try/catch since @Res() bypasses NestJS
   exception filters. Errors now return proper JSON responses instead
   of hanging.

2. Explicitly set isServiceToken=false in API key and session auth
   handlers to prevent accidental fallthrough in PermissionGuard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): validate domain format in trust portal to prevent path injection

Add domain format validation in addCustomDomain and checkDnsRecords to
ensure user-provided domain values can't inject path segments into
Vercel API or DNS lookup URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): merge duplicate permissions, skip chat audit, add maxSteps, fix control mapping descriptions

1. PermissionGuard: merge actions for duplicate resources instead of
   overwriting, preventing silently dropped permission checks.

2. Assistant chat completions: add @SkipAuditLog() to prevent noisy
   "Created app" audit entries on every chat message.

3. Assistant chat: add maxSteps=5 to streamText so the model can
   synthesize tool results into natural language responses.

4. Audit log resolvers: extract resource name from URL path instead
   of hardcoding "policy". fetchControlIds now dynamically resolves
   the correct Prisma model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: rBAC support and major security improvements

Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system with custom roles,
permission guards, and audit logging across the platform.

Key changes:
- Hybrid auth guard for session, API key, service token
- Permission guard with better-auth and custom roles
- Granular resource:action permissions
- Audit log interceptor with mutation tracking
- API key prefix indexing for O(1) lookups
- Service token org existence validation
- Domain validation to prevent SSRF
- Streaming endpoint error handling
- AI assistant chat with permission-gated tools

BREAKING CHANGE: all API endpoints now require RBAC
permissions via @RequirePermission decorators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Update Rail Transfer section to use categoryHint field for storage identification

- Add railTransferStorages() helper function that identifies storages with
  'transfer' in their categoryHint field
- Update all Rail Transfer section functions to use railTransferStorages()
  instead of storagesByFamily('TRANSF')
- This allows Rail Transfer storages to hold cement products while still
  being recognized as rail transfer operations

Rail Transfer storages are now identified by:
  Storage -> Settings -> Hint field containing 'transfer'

This matches the schema where:
- BRS / TRANSF / BRS IL-BULK = Rail Transfer storage (marked with 'transfer' hint)
- BRS IL-BULK = Cement product stored in the rail transfer storage
- BRSRAIL = Equipment that loads rail cars
- BRS/SWITCH = Equipment/event for rail company pickup

Fix Vercel serverless crashes: run vercel commands from monorepo root

After Turborepo migration, npm workspace hoisting moved packages to root
node_modules/, but vercel build ran from app subdirectories where the
bundler couldn't find them. Switch from working-directory to --cwd so
Vercel's nft bundler can traverse the full monorepo. Also add null-safety
filter in memberships endpoint for broken forum foreign keys.

commit.message:
Fix low result count in multi-area searches

Two bugs fixed:
1. Query was "businessType in city" + rectangle bias — conflicting signals.
   Now uses just businessType when locationBias is active.
2. locationRestriction (hard filter) replaced with locationBias (soft preference)
   so businesses on cell borders are not excluded. Cross-cell duplicates are
   already handled by place ID deduplication in the client.
3. locationBias not re-sent on paginated requests (pageToken re-uses original params).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: lead-scraper-google-maps
repository.description: Business lead scraper using the official Google Places API
